# Edge Guardian

> Edge Guardian is a free, open-source, single-binary edge guardian for Linux. From one access-log tail it does two things: security — detect scanners, brute-force and exploit attempts and ban the source IPs at the nftables firewall — and edge health — watch each site's 5xx ratio, latency and request rate and alert when a site is degraded or down. It alerts via Telegram or email and ships with a built-in local dashboard. One line to install.

Edge Guardian turns one log stream into two branches that run side by side: the security branch matches bad traffic and bans the source IP at the kernel via nftables; the health branch counts per-site status, latency and request rate and alerts on trouble (with sustained + cooldown so it does not spam). It is a single static Go binary with no database, no agent, and no external service. It starts in dry-run mode (detect and alert without blocking) so operators can trust it before arming it.

Positioning: simpler than fail2ban (single host, regex-heavy, no dashboard) and lighter than CrowdSec (agent + local API + bouncer + collections, paid dashboard). Beyond security, Edge Guardian also answers "is a site degraded or down?" — something neither fail2ban nor CrowdSec does. Its free tier includes the dashboard and the health monitoring. It deliberately does NOT do web analytics (no unique visitors, referrers, or top pages): it answers "is anything wrong?", not "who is viewing what?".

## Key facts

- License: Apache-2.0 (free and open source, including the dashboard and health monitoring)
- Language: Go, distributed as a single static binary (cross-compiles for any Linux)
- Requirements: Linux with nftables (Debian 11+, Ubuntu 20.04+, RHEL 9+) and root or CAP_NET_ADMIN
- Enforcement: bans IPs in an nftables set with auto-expiring timeouts; escalating bans for repeat offenders
- Security detectors: HTTP scanner paths, SSH brute-force, exploit signatures (SQLi / path traversal / RCE probes / Log4Shell), bad-bot User-Agents (sqlmap, nikto, nuclei, masscan), rate-abuse / DoS-lite (per-IP request flood), port scan (distinct-port counting), honeypot ports
- Edge health: per-site status mix, 5xx error ratio, request rate and p95 latency; alerts on degraded / down / recovered; a /sites dashboard page
- Threat intel: imports public blocklists (FireHOL, Spamhaus DROP) into an nftables interval set
- Alerts: Telegram and email (via Resend) for both bans and health, enriched with country/ASN from offline GeoIP
- Dashboard: embedded local web UI (Chi + templ + HTMX) with a live "Sentinel line", ban ledger, live feed, and a Sites health page
- Safety: CIDR allowlist and dry-run mode to avoid self-lockout; exploit/bad-bot/rate-abuse/health are off by default
- Logs: nginx combined format, or the recommended nginx JSON format for full per-site health (host + latency)

## Install

- One-line install (Linux, root): `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sondt/edge-guardian/main/install.sh | sudo bash`
- Packages: .deb (Debian/Ubuntu) and .rpm (RHEL/Rocky/Fedora) for amd64 and arm64, from GitHub Releases
- Docker: `docker run -d --network host --cap-add NET_ADMIN ghcr.io/sondt/edge-guardian`

## Links

- Source code and releases: https://github.com/sondt/edge-guardian
- Configuration reference (every key documented inline): https://github.com/sondt/edge-guardian/blob/main/config.example.toml
- Contributing: https://github.com/sondt/edge-guardian/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
- Security policy: https://github.com/sondt/edge-guardian/blob/main/SECURITY.md
- License: https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
